summary: Stereo‑rich SBD capture of a legendary late‑72 show, highlighted by a scorching Dark Star→El Paso, massive Playing in the Band and standout China Cat → The Rider performances.
review: The recording is an excellent‑sounding SBD remix that delivers unusually clear stereo imaging, making every jam feel spacious. Musically the concert is widely praised – three strong sets packed with a monster Dark Star→El Paso, soaring Playing in the Band, fiery China Cat → The Rider and several superb renditions of Bird Song, Greatest Show on Earth and El Paso. Even the few minor flaws (a slightly out‑of‑tune Mexicali) can’t dent the overall brilliance.

ai_rating.rationale: Overwhelmingly positive comments on both performance and audio quality across eight reviews; only one moderate critique, yielding a high combined rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Listeners note the version sounds very “stereo,” crisp, and well‑balanced; a dankseed remix of the SBD source is praised for clarity with no reported dropouts or major issues.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Three tightly‑structured sets that build momentum: a high‑energy first set that kicks off with Sugaree and China Cat → The Rider, a jam‑heavy second set featuring Dark Star→El Paso and Playing in the Band, and a concise encore that caps the night without excessive meat‑head extensions.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeated praise for his soaring tone, inventive solos (especially on Dark Star and El Paso) and overall guitar leadership.
band_member_comments.Phil: Solo in Dark Star highlighted as “groovy” and a key moment of the jam.
band_member_comments.Bob: Vocal intro on Playing in the Band noted for its heart; rhythm work praised throughout, especially on Bird Song and Jack Straw.
band_member_comments.Drums: Billy’s drumming described as powerful and driving the jams, with overall solid percussion throughout.
